title: 14. Multi Persona Roleplay
description: A guided setup for MPRC story mode: master story drafts, focused cast turns, multi-voice output, Story Images, Story Memory, Spotify music, remote play, Chromecast, and troubleshooting.

steps.title: Open Roleplay
steps.body: Open the Roleplay workspace tab. This addon is separate from normal main chat and has its own Story Health, Play, Master, Registry, Voice, Story Images, Story Sounds, Story Memory, Guide, and Troubleshooting sections. Use Read steps aloud or Read Step if you want spoken guidance while switching sections.

steps.title: Start With Story Health
steps.body: Story Health is the safest first stop. Start Demo / Validate / Continue loads a known-good demo, checks narrator lock, voices, Story Images, Story Sounds, story memory, and then queues a Continue turn so you can prove the whole loop before editing.

steps.title: Pick the Active Cast
steps.body: Registry manages the cast. Keep one master narrator and only enable the personas that should participate in the current story. A tighter cast gives the story director a cleaner speaker pool and reduces character blending.

steps.title: Create a Master Story Draft
steps.body: The Master Story prompt turns a single rough idea into a draft with premise, session state, AR state, cast, visual direction, and story metadata. Review the generated JSON before Apply Draft so broken names, voices, and scene goals do not become the new baseline.

steps.title: Export Story Packages
steps.body: Story Library Export can package the story, selected personas, voice samples, memory, AudioFX, and visual assets. Use it after a setup works, not before, so imported stories bring useful data instead of unfinished experiments.

steps.title: Assign Persona Voices
steps.body: Voice assigns samples and backend overrides per persona. The multi-voice output works best when the active TTS backend supports reference audio and each speaking persona has a clear voice sample.

steps.title: Use Play as the Live Story Surface
steps.body: Play is the live session surface. Start Play, Stop, Restart Story, Clear, Float Play, Voice, Intent, Focus, and Advanced stay together so the story can be driven without hunting through setup tabs.

steps.title: Use Focused Speaker Deliberately
steps.body: The Focus selector is the practical cast-mode control. Choose a specific character for focused speaker turns when you want less blending and stronger dialogue. Use broader joined-cast behavior for setup, recap, or master-story style turns.

steps.title: Keep Structured Output On for Multi-TTS
steps.body: Structured Output asks the model for clean narrator and character segments. That gives voice routing a safer contract than guessing from loose paragraphs. Export Structured LLM Output is useful when debugging bad segmentation.

steps.title: Read the Story Director Summary
steps.body: The Story Director summary shows the current premise/state, focused cast mode, pacing, choice nudge, speaker discipline, and structured-output state. If the story starts wandering, this is the first summary to inspect.

steps.title: Check the Visual Beat
steps.body: Story Images and Visual Beat explain what Visual Reply should draw: the actual scene beat, subject, mood, location, and image intent. Use OpenAI, Grok/xAI, Runware, or another active image provider according to the story's needs. The beat should describe the moment the player cares about, not a generic summary of the whole story.

steps.title: Let Spotify Sense Handle Story Music
steps.body: Spotify Music shows the soundtrack request sent to Spotify Sense. Use it for ambient or story music that follows the scene mood, with fades and lower-music-while-speaking behavior handled by Spotify Sense so TTS remains understandable.

steps.title: Use Remote and Chromecast After Local Play Works
steps.body: Advanced includes LAN Remote and Chromecast controls. Keep local Play working first, then test the phone app or cast target. The remote code is a pairing code; do not treat it like a public internet password.

steps.title: Troubleshoot the Exact Payloads
steps.body: Troubleshooting shows the effective prompt, Visual Reply prompt, Voice Routing Check, compact state, and Visual Reply call log. Use it when a voice is wrong, an image misses the scene, memory feels stale, or Spotify music does not match the story.
